# Results

## Pipeline Implementation

The meta-pipe pipeline comprises 12 skill modules, 24 Python automation scripts (~3,600 lines of code), 5 R analysis scripts, and 74 methodology reference documents.
The complete source code is available as an open-source repository.
<!-- TODO: add repository URL and Zenodo DOI -->

## Demonstration: Literature Search and Study Selection

<!-- TODO: Insert PRISMA flow diagram (Figure 2) -->

The systematic search identified [N] records from PubMed and Scopus.
After deduplication and title/abstract screening (inter-rater kappa = [value]), [N] full-text articles were assessed for eligibility.
Five randomized controlled trials met the inclusion criteria: KEYNOTE-522, IMpassion031, GeparNuevo, NeoTRIPaPDL1, and CamRelief, enrolling a total of 2,402 patients.

## Study Characteristics

<!-- TODO: Insert Table 3 -->

The five included trials were published between 2019 and 2024.
Sample sizes ranged from 174 to 1,174 participants.
Three trials evaluated pembrolizumab, one evaluated atezolizumab, and one evaluated camrelizumab.
All trials compared immune checkpoint inhibitor plus neoadjuvant chemotherapy versus chemotherapy alone in patients with early-stage triple-negative breast cancer.

## Efficacy Outcomes

### Pathologic Complete Response (Primary Outcome)

The addition of immune checkpoint inhibitors to neoadjuvant chemotherapy was associated with a significantly higher rate of pathologic complete response (RR 1.26, 95% CI 1.16-1.37, p=0.0015; I^2^=0%, 5 trials, N=2,402; @fig-forest).
The quality of evidence was rated HIGH by GRADE assessment.
The number needed to treat was 7 (95% CI 5-11).

<!-- TODO: Insert Figure 3 (forest plot) -->

### Event-Free Survival

Event-free survival at 5 years favored the immune checkpoint inhibitor group (HR 0.66, 95% CI 0.51-0.86, p=0.021).
The quality of evidence was rated MODERATE by GRADE assessment, downgraded for imprecision due to the limited number of trials reporting this outcome.

### Overall Survival

Overall survival at 5 years was reported in two trials, both showing significant benefit (HR 0.48).
The quality of evidence was rated LOW, downgraded for imprecision and incomplete reporting.

## Safety Outcomes

Serious adverse events were more frequent in the immune checkpoint inhibitor group (RR 1.50, 95% CI 1.13-1.98; NNH=10).
Grade 3 or higher immune-related adverse events increased approximately 8.5-fold.
Fatal adverse events occurred in 0.40% of the intervention group versus 0% of the control group (NNH=250).

## Time and Quality Metrics

The complete pipeline execution required approximately 14 hours (@tbl-time), compared with an estimated 100 or more hours for manual conduct.
The publication readiness score reached 95-98%, with PRISMA compliance at 27 of 27 items.
The final manuscript comprised 4,921 words with 31 citations, 7 tables, and 5 figures.

<!-- TODO: Insert Table 4 (time breakdown) -->

## Comparison with Existing Tools

<!-- TODO: Insert Table 2 (feature comparison matrix) -->

@tbl-comparison presents a feature comparison of meta-pipe with six existing SR/MA automation tools.
meta-pipe is the only tool providing coverage across all 10 pipeline stages, including statistical analysis, manuscript generation, and automated quality assurance.
